channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: [AINews] Google I/O 2026: Gemini 3.5 Flash, Omni (NanoBanana for Video), Spark (background agents), and Antigravity 2.0
item.description: Google has been busy!

item.title: Moonshot AI Launches Kimi WebBridge: AI Agents That Browse the Web Like You
item.description: Moonshot AI releases Kimi WebBridge, a browser extension that lets the Kimi AI agent operate with your full browser identity — your logged-in sessions, cookies, and accounts — to perform tasks autonomously on your behalf.

item.title: Willand CEO: After Selling 1 Million Robotic Lawn Mowers, It's Time to Make Myself 'Unimportant'
item.description: Willand CEO Ren Guanjiao reflects on seven years building boundary-free robotic lawn mowers, now shipping 1M units annually, and explains why his next goal is to make himself operationally redundant as the company scales.

item.title: AutoAgents.ai Raises Pre-A Round to Build Digital Labor Platform for Knowledge Workers
item.description: AutoAgents.ai, a Hangzhou-based AI agent startup founded by former Alibaba DAMO Academy researchers, closes a Pre-A round to build an intelligent platform that automates knowledge work — positioning itself as a 'digital labor factory.'

item.title: Chance AI Raises Millions in Angel Round Led by Meitu, NYX Ventures
item.description: Visual AI startup Chance AI announces a multi-million dollar angel round led by Meitu, with participation from NYX Ventures and Alibaba-affiliated investors, aiming to expand its camera-first AI product to North America.

item.title: Xynova Unveils Hybrid-Driven Gen-2 Dexterous Hand for Humanoid Robots
item.description: Xynova releases its second-generation hybrid-driven dexterous hand, designed to give humanoid robots human-like manipulation intelligence for real-world task execution.

item.title: API users may see increased error rates for GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.5
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Chat Completions (Operational)

item.title: Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector
item.description: Verizon’s 2026 DBIR finds vulnerability exploitation has overtaken credential abuse as the leading breach vector, as AI accelerates attacks, patching delays worsen, and ransomware and third-party compromises continue to surge. The post Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes...

item.title: Cloudflare Access AI control - MCP server creation impacted
item.description: May 19, 23:37 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 19, 22:09 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate this issue.May 19, 21:59 UTCInvestigating - Customers may experience issue adding new MCP Servers or authenticating existing ones through the Zero Trust Dash

item.title: Roundtables: Inside the Musk v. Altman Trial
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI, in which he alleged CEO Sam Altman and President Greg Brockman had deceived him over the company’s non-profit status. Watch as AI reporter and attorney Michelle Kim, who covered the trial for MIT Technology Review, joins...

item.title: Intermittent Cloudflare Dashboard SSO login failures with Okta
item.description: May 19, 20:10 UTCResolved - Cloudflare engineering has ruled out any service degrading within Cloudflare systems. Customers are advised to review their Okta authentication logs for potential errors. A status update will be provided if Cloudflare Engineering determines the specific Okta issue...

item.title: OlmoEarth v1.1: A more efficient family of models
Cloudflare Dashboard and Cloudflare API service issues
item.description: May 19, 16:48 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 19, 15:54 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 19, 15:36 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Cloudflare Dashboard and related APIs. These issues do not affect the serving...

item.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ad fraud and malvertising operation dubbed Trapdoor targeting Android device users. The activity, per HUMAN's Satori Threat Intelligence and Research Team, encompassed 455 malicious Android apps and 183 threat actor-owned command-and-control...

item.title: Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation
item.description: Drupal says attackers may develop an exploit for the vulnerability within hours or days. The post Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Microsoft Disrupts Malware-Signing Service Run by ‘Fox Tempest’
item.description: Fox Tempest provides a service that cybercriminals use to distribute ransomware and other malware disguised as legitimate software. The post Microsoft Disrupts Malware-Signing Service Run by ‘Fox Tempest’ appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Digital Experience in Cloudflare Zero Trust Latency
item.description: May 19, 15:25 UTCInvestigating - Actions related to Digital Experience are delayed, Cloudflare is aware and investigating.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 19, 15:21 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to monitor for any further issues.May 19, 15:19 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 19, 14:03 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: DirtyDecrypt PoC Released for Linux Kernel CVE-2026-31635 LPE Vulnerability
item.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has now been released for a recently patched security flaw in the Linux kernel that could allow for local privilege escalation (LPE). Dubbed DirtyDecrypt (aka DirtyCBC), the vulnerability was discovered and reported by the Zellic and V12 security team on May 9,...

item.title: Understanding the modern cybercrime landscape
item.description: Throughout 2025, HPE observed significant changes in how cybercriminals operate. Analyzing real-world threats, our HPE Threat Labs highlighted an industrialization of the cyber criminals’ methods in its new In the Wild Report, enabling greater scale, speed and structure in their campaigns. They...

item.title: Legacy Windows Tool MSHTA Fuels Surge in Silent Malware Attacks
item.description: Attackers are increasingly abusing Microsoft’s decades-old MSHTA utility to stealthily deliver stealers, loaders, and persistent malware through phishing, fake software downloads, and LOLBIN-based attack chains. The post Legacy Windows Tool MSHTA Fuels Surge in Silent Malware Attacks appeared first...

item.title: Unpatched ChromaDB Vulnerability Can Lead to Server Takeover
item.description: The security defect can be exploited remotely, without authentication, to execute arbitrary code and leak sensitive information. The post Unpatched ChromaDB Vulnerability Can Lead to Server Takeover appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: Musk v. Altman, smart glasses for warfare, and Google I/O
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Here’s why Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against OpenAI, which centered on whether the company breached its...

item.title: Colossal Biosciences is growing chickens in a 3D-printed artificial eggshell
item.description: The baby chicks were shifting and starting to pip—or trying to hatch. But not from an egg.  Instead, these chickens were growing inside transparent 3D-printed plastic cups at the Dallas headquarters of Colossal Biosciences. The biotech company today claimed it has developed a “fully artificial...

item.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
item.description: In February 2026, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform called EvilTokens went live. Within five weeks, it had compromised more than 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries.  The targets of the platform received a message asking them to enter a short code at...

item.title: Workers AI availability issues
item.description: May 19, 11:09 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 19, 10:56 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating availability issues with several workers AI models.

item.title: Drupal to Release Urgent Core Security Updates on May 20, Sites Told to Prepare
item.description: Drupal has issued an alert stating that it intends to release a "core security release" for all supported branches on May 20, 2026, from 5-9 p.m. UTC. "The Drupal Security Team urges you to reserve time for core updates at that time because exploits might be developed within hours or days," the...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 19, 10:08 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 19, 08:41 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway Vulnerabilities Enable RCE and Mail Traffic Access
item.description: Critical security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway, an enterprise-grade email security solution, that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution and enable an attacker to read arbitrary mails from the virtual appliance. "These vulnerabilities could have...

item.title: [AINews] How to land a job at a frontier lab (on Pretraining)
item.description: a quiet day before google i/o lets us amplify a notable blogpost

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: May 19, 07:00 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai and Claude Code login
item.description: May 19, 04:51 UTCResolved - This issue has been resolved.May 19, 04:46 UTCMonitoring - From 04:34–04:42 UTC, we saw elevated errors and latency on requests within the Claude.ai applications. Additionally, users may have experienced errors when attempting to login, including authenticating Claude...

item.title: Solvita: Enhancing Large Language Models for Competitive Programming via Agentic Evolution
item.description: arXiv:2605.15301v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) still struggle with the rigorous reasoning demands of hard competitive programming. While recent multi-agent frameworks attempt to bridge this reliability gap, they remain fundamentally stateless: they rely on static...

item.title: SMCEvolve: Principled Scientific Discovery via Sequential Monte Carlo Evolution
item.description: arXiv:2605.15308v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-driven program evolution has emerged as a powerful tool for automated scientific discovery, yet existing frameworks offer no principled guide for designing their individual components and provide no guarantee that the search converges. We...

item.title: Context Pruning for Coding Agents via Multi-Rubric Latent Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2605.15315v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-powered coding agents spend the majority of their token budget reading repository files, yet much of the retrieved code is irrelevant to the task at hand. Existing learned pruners compress this context with a single-objective sequence labeler,...

item.title: Zero-Shot Goal Recognition with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.15333v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models have recently reached near-parity with classical planners on well-known planning domains, yet this competence relies on world-knowledge exploitation rather than genuine symbolic reasoning. Goal recognition is a complementary...

item.title: Belief Engine: Configurable and Inspectable Stance Dynamics in Multi-Agent LLM Deliberation
item.description: arXiv:2605.15343v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based agents are increasingly used to simulate deliberative interactions such as negotiation, conflict resolution, and multi-turn opinion exchange. Yet generated transcripts often do not reveal why an agent's stance changes: movement may reflect...

item.title: Systematic Optimization of Real-Time Diffusion Model Inference on Apple M3 Ultra
item.description: arXiv:2605.16259v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While real-time image generation using diffusion models has advanced rapidly on NVIDIA GPUs, systematic optimization research on non-CUDA platforms such as Apple Silicon remains extremely limited. In this study, we conducted comprehensive optimization...

item.title: Mirror Descent-Type Algorithms for the Variational Inequality Problem with Functional Constraints
item.description: arXiv:2605.16262v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Variational inequalities play a key role in machine learning research, such as generative adversarial networks, reinforcement learning, adversarial training, and generative models. This paper is devoted to the constrained variational inequality...

item.title: Reducing Credit Assignment Variance via Counterfactual Reasoning Paths
item.description: arXiv:2605.16302v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning for multi-step reasoning with large language models (LLMs) often relies on sparse terminal rewards, leading to poor credit assignment conditions where the final feedback is evenly propagated across all intermediate decisions....

item.title: SignMuon: Communication-Efficient Distributed Muon Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2605.16311v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Distributed training of large neural networks is bottlenecked by full-precision gradient communication and by coordinatewise optimizers that ignore the matrix structure of weight tensors. We propose Sign-Muon, a 1-bit, matrix-aware optimizer that...

item.title: When Actions Disappear: Adversarial Action Removal in Self-Play Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.16312v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study adversarial action masking in self-play reinforcement learning: an attacker selectively removes legal actions from a victim's action set. Unlike observation or action perturbations, removal eliminates decision options before the agent acts....

item.title: The Scaling Laws of Skills in LLM Agent Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.16508v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As agent systems scale, skills accumulate into large reusable libraries, yet their scaling laws remain poorly understood. Across 15 frontier LLMs, 1,141 real-world skills, and over 3M routing or execution decisions, we identify two coupled laws....

item.title: PQR: A Framework to Generate Diverse and Realistic User Queries that Elicit QA Agent Failures
item.description: arXiv:2605.16551v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating LLM-based agents remains challenging because identifying meaningful failure cases often requires substantial human effort to design realistic test scenarios. Prior works primarily focus on automatically discovering agent failures induced by...

item.title: Scaling Accessible Mathematics on arXiv: HTML Conversion and MathML 4
item.description: arXiv:2605.16562v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We report on the ongoing development of arXiv's HTML Papers offering, available on every new TeX/LaTeX submission since its initial release in 2023. The main highlights from 2025 and early 2026 are: (i) community-driven improvements to HTML fidelity...

item.title: Beyond Sentiment Classification: A Generative Framework for Emotion Intensity Evaluation in Text
item.description: arXiv:2605.16613v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce a novel approach to emotion modeling that shifts the focus from identification to evaluation, addressing the limitations of discrete classification in applied domains such as finance. By constructing a dataset of emotional intensity...

item.title: SKG-Eval: Stateful Evaluation of Multi-Turn Dialogue via Incremental Semantic Knowledge Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2605.16650v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating multi-turn dialogue systems remains challenging because response quality depends not only on the current prompt, but also on previously established entities, claims, and conversational commitments. Existing automatic evaluators, including...

item.title: Here’s why Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI
item.description: On Monday, the jury in Musk v. Altman dealt Elon Musk a major blow—reaching a unanimous advisory verdict that he had sued OpenAI too late and, as a result, his claims are barred by the applicable statutes of limitations. US District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers immediately accepted it.  Musk...

item.title: Introducing the Ettin Reranker Family
Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 18, 22:19 UTCResolved - The issue affecting Claude services has been resolved. Impact occurred from 21:47 to 22:18 UTC.May 18, 22:10 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating elevated errors on requests to Claude Opus 4.7. We will provide an update as soon as possible.
